items.text: As we refinance debt or renew derivatives, we are exposed to movement in global interest rates and rate differentials.
items.rationale: Restates existing interest rate/derivative exposure risk in a general way without adding new specifics.

items.text: Tax Act extended or made permanent many of the corporate tax changes arising under the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act passed in 2017 (the 2017 U.S.
items.rationale: Descriptive statement about prior tax law context, not a new risk itself.

items.text: We do not anticipate the 2025 U.S.
items.rationale: Statement of expectation/non-anticipation about 2025 tax act, framing language rather than a substantive risk.

items.text: The Procter & Gamble Company 9 In December 2021, the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) issued “Pillar Two” model rules which established a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15% for large multinational corporations.
items.rationale: Introduces specific new global minimum tax framework (Pillar Two) that could materially affect the company, not previously mentioned.

items.text: Many countries have implemented or are in the process of implementing Pillar Two legislation into their respective domestic laws.
items.rationale: Elaborates on the scope of Pillar Two implementation across countries, expanding on the newly introduced risk.

items.text: As the Pillar Two global minimum tax and other tax laws and related regulations are revised, enacted and implemented, a material impact to our financial condition, results of operations, cash flows or effective tax rate may occur.
items.rationale: Specifies potential material financial impact from Pillar Two and related tax law changes, expanding the newly introduced risk with concrete consequences.
